description: Dialogue for Captain Roderick Steele, leader of the Iron Company mercenary guild in Dalhurst

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: State your business. The Iron Company doesn't have time for idle chatter.

choices.text: I want to join the Iron Company.
Tell me about the Iron Company.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Soldier. Good to see you back at the Hall. The Company always has work for those willing to bleed for it.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: The Iron Company isn't some tavern gang looking for warm bodies. We're professionals. Soldiers. Every man and woman here has proven themselves in blood. You want in? You'll have to do the same.

choices.text: What do I need to do?
What makes the Iron Company different from other mercenaries?
I've killed plenty. That's not enough?

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Other sellswords fight for coin and run when the odds turn. The Iron Company fights to win. We train together. We hold formation. We don't abandon contracts, and we don't abandon each other. That's why nobles pay triple our rate - because we deliver.

choices.text: I can respect that. What's the test?
Sounds like a lot of rules.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Rules keep soldiers alive. Discipline wins battles. If you can't handle structure, find a bandit gang to join. They don't have standards either.

choices.text: Fair enough. What's the enlistment test?

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Any thug can kill. What matters is how you kill, when you kill, and whether you can follow orders while doing it. A soldier who can't take commands is a liability. Prove you can fight smart, not just fight.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Then you're not Company material. We fight as a unit. That's non-negotiable. Come back when you understand what that means.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: We've got a goblin raiding party that's been hitting supply lines east of Dalhurst. Standard vermin, but organized. Take them out - all of them, including their warchief. Bring back the warchief's battle standard as proof. Do that alone and without dying, and you'll have earned your place.

choices.text: How many goblins are we talking?

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Reports say a dozen, maybe fifteen. Warchief leads them - bigger than the rest, wears human bones as trophies. They've killed three merchant guards this week. Standard goblin tactics: ambush, swarm, retreat if things go bad. Don't let them retreat.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: 150 gold and Company membership. After that, you get access to real contracts - ones that pay ten times that. Plus barracks access, equipment at cost, and brothers who'll watch your back. That's worth more than gold.

choices.text: I'm in. Where's this goblin camp?
I need to think about it.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: The goblins are camped in the hills east of Dalhurst, near the old watchtower ruins. Kill them all, take the warchief's standard, and return here. Don't come back until it's done. And soldier - don't embarrass me.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: You're back. The goblins?

choices.text: Dead. All of them. Here's the warchief's standard.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Clean work. The merchants will breathe easier, and that's one less goblin warchief breeding more problems. Here's your pay - 150 gold. And this... this is your Company insignia. Wear it with honor. You're Iron Company now, soldier. Don't make me regret it.

choices.text: I won't let you down, Captain.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: See that you don't. The barracks are open to you now. Rest up, train with the others, and check the contract board when you're ready for work. We'll make a proper soldier of you yet.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Eager. Good. We've got caravan escorts, guard details, and a few tactical operations that need capable hands. Check the contract board in the hall. Start with the lower-tier work and prove yourself. Rank and pay come with experience.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Then why are you here wasting my time? Get back out there and finish the job. The Company doesn't pay for partial results.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: The contract board has everything we're currently hiring for. Escort missions, monster hunts, tactical operations. Pick what suits your skills. Payment on completion, no exceptions.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: You've proven yourself reliable. There's a noble house that needs discrete protection during some... politically sensitive negotiations. High pay, higher risk. Talk to Lieutenant Garret at the officer's quarters if you're interested.

choices.text: I'll stick to the board for now.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: The Iron Company has operated for sixty years. Founded by Captain Aldric Iron - my predecessor - after the Tenger Wars. We're the best professional fighting force money can buy. Not thugs, not murderers. Soldiers. We take contracts, we complete them, we get paid. Simple as that.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Caravan protection. Monster extermination. Siege support. Tactical strikes. Anything that requires trained fighters who won't cut and run. We don't do assassinations, and we don't break contracts. That's why clients trust us.

choices.text: No assassinations? What if the target deserves it?
What happens if you break a contract?

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: We're soldiers, not murderers. There's a difference. Killing a man in battle is war. Killing him in his bed is cowardice. The Company has standards. If you want that kind of work, find the Thieves Guild. They'll accommodate your... flexibility.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Good. Standards matter. They're what separate us from the rabble. Was there anything else?

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Then the Company isn't for you. We don't need members who'll do anything for coin. Find work elsewhere.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: In sixty years, the Iron Company has never broken a contract. Not once. That reputation is worth more than gold. A member who abandons a contract is expelled. Permanently. We don't give second chances for cowardice.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Thirty-two years in the Company. Started as a recruit, same as everyone else. Fought in the Border Wars, the Goblin Purge, the Thornfield Rebellion. Lost count of the battles, lost count of the friends. But I'm still here, and so is the Company. That's what matters.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Captain Aldric Iron chose me himself, fifteen years back. Said I understood what the Company meant - not just the fighting, but the brotherhood. The discipline. He died three months later. Arrow to the throat at the Siege of Blackmont. I've led the Company ever since.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Every day. Now, was there something else you needed, soldier?

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Command always is. But someone has to carry it. I'd rather it be me than someone who'd run the Company into the ground. Now, enough sentiment. Back to work.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: The Thornfield Rebellion. Twelve years ago. We were hired to break a siege, found ourselves outnumbered five to one. Lost forty good soldiers in three days of fighting. But we held, and we won. That's what the Company does - we hold.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: The Company stands strong. Two hundred active soldiers, contracts coming in steady. Could always use more capable fighters though. The realm's getting more dangerous - more monsters, more conflict. Good for business, bad for everyone else.

choices.text: What's the biggest threat right now?

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: The Tengers are pushing south again. Goblins are bolder than they've been in years. And there are rumors of something worse stirring in the east - creatures that shouldn't exist. Dark times ahead. The Company will be busy.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Then don't waste my time. Come back when you're serious.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Dismissed.

nodes.speaker: Captain Steele
nodes.text: Stay sharp, soldier. The Company needs you ready.
